zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Stewart於1575年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾奧爾崔時，她的父親Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree27歲，母親Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland30歲。她於1595年與Sir George Morrison Jr在蘇格蘭藍納可爾格拉斯哥結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於1650年在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利享年75歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Stewart于1575年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾奧爾崔时，她的父亲Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree27岁，母亲Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland30岁。她于1595年与Sir George Morrison Jr在苏格兰藍納可爾格拉斯哥结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。她去世于1650年在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利享年75岁，埋葬在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Stewart was born in 1575, in Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree, was 27 and her mother, Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland, was 30. She married Sir George Morrison Jr in 1595, in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died in 1650, in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, at the age of 75, and was buried in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Stewart est née en 1575, à Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir George Morrison Jr en 1595, à Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Elle est décédée en 1650, à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

Sie hat 1595 in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland Sir George Morrison Jr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart(이)가 1575년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 오치트리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1595년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 글라스고에서 Sir George Morrison Jr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Stewart родилась в 1575 г. в местности очилтри, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir George Morrison Jr в 1595 г. в местности гласгоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Она умерла в 1650 г. в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.
